message:
test(fcp-core): pin SchemaId hash + canonical-form stability (sbget)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/schema_id_hash_display_stability.rs.
Bead asks for "SchemaId hash + Display+FromStr roundtrip"; SchemaId
does NOT implement Display or FromStr — the canonical form is
produced by `as_bytes()` returning the documented
`{namespace}:{name}@{version}` shape. Tests pin what exists.

21 tests cover:

- Hash trait determinism via DefaultHasher; equal SchemaIds (built
  via new / try_new / struct-literal / clone) hash to the same u64.
- Distinct canonical schemas hash distinctly in practice.
- as_bytes() exact format `fcp.core:AuditEvent@1.0.0`, including
  pre-release and build-metadata version forms.
- SchemaHash determinism, fixed 32-byte length, lowercase hex
  Display, round-trip via from_bytes.
- Distinct schemas and distinct versions produce distinct
  SchemaHashes (1.0.0 ≠ 1.0.0-rc.1, 1.0.0 ≠ 1.0.1, etc.).
- Length-prefix injectivity: SchemaIds built via the public-fields
  bypass with reserved separators in different positions
  (`("a:b","c")` vs `("a","b:c")`, `("a@b","c")` vs `("a","b@c")`,
  `("","ab")` vs `("a","b")`) hash differently.
- try_new rejects `:` and `@` in both namespace and name with the
  offending field+separator carried in ReservedSeparator.
- SchemaId::new (the panic constructor) panics on reserved
  separators.
- HashMap-key correctness via fresh-construction lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
